introSection: The Cú Chulainn Coaster is a wooden roller coaster located at Emerald Park in Ashbourne, County Meath, Ireland.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
The Cú Chulainn Coaster was officially opened on 6 June 2015. It surpassed Coaster Express at Parque Warner Madrid in San Martín de la Vega, Community of Madrid, Spain in ride length as The Cú Chulainn Coaster's ride time is 3 minutes while Coaster Express is only 2 minutes.

It was launched as "the longest rollercoaster in Europe and the only wooden rollercoaster with an inversion". Despite this claim, there is no inversion, which is instead a overbanked turn at 115 degrees. The coaster reached a height of 32 meters and a speed of 90 km/h.

The Cú Chulainn Coaster is the 17th coaster manufactured from The Gravity Group, LLC. It was built by The Gravitykraft Corporation.

sections.title: Ride Experience
sections.content: The train leaves the station into a small drop and a 90 degree turn before heading into the chain lift which climbs up to 32 meters before dropping 32.2 and hitting 3 Gs and the bottom of the hill.

sections.title: External links
sections.content: * The Cú Chulainn Coaster on the park's website

fullText:
The Cú Chulainn Coaster is a wooden roller coaster located at Emerald Park in Ashbourne, County Meath, Ireland.

The Cú Chulainn Coaster was officially opened on 6 June 2015. It surpassed Coaster Express at Parque Warner Madrid in San Martín de la Vega, Community of Madrid, Spain in ride length as The Cú Chulainn Coaster's ride time is 3 minutes while Coaster Express is only 2 minutes.

It was launched as "the longest rollercoaster in Europe and the only wooden rollercoaster with an inversion". Despite this claim, there is no inversion, which is instead a overbanked turn at 115 degrees. The coaster reached a height of 32 meters and a speed of 90 km/h.

The Cú Chulainn Coaster is the 17th coaster manufactured from The Gravity Group, LLC. It was built by The Gravitykraft Corporation.



The train leaves the station into a small drop and a 90 degree turn before heading into the chain lift which climbs up to 32 meters before dropping 32.2 and hitting 3 Gs and the bottom of the hill.


 * The Cú Chulainn Coaster on the park's website
